blocks.content: The kings stood in stunned silence, gazing up at the host of angels filling the sky.

blocks.speaker: Rumors
blocks.content: So it's true…
Rumors that Heaven's Army had disappeared from Jive's borders had circulated, but no one had expected this.

blocks.speaker: Rumors
blocks.content: They've aligned with angels?
While humans have been known to bargain with even spirits for what they desire, this revelation was shocking.

blocks.speaker: Rumors
blocks.content: Could negotiations have been possible? That many angels could obliterate an entire nation.
Satiel's piercing gaze scanned the delegations of each nation.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: Where is Ikael?
She didn't see her among the Nephilim.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: Could they have hidden him already? No, not while I'm here…
Her eyes sharpened, and she suddenly froze.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: Satan!
Gustav Havitz.
Though his appearance had changed since the battle in Heaven, the essence of Satan's presence was unmistakable.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: How dare you show your face here…?
Sensing Satiel's rising fury, Havitz showed his first sign of interest.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Oh? An angel?
He climbed onto a saddle, turned to face Satiel, and wrapped an arm around Wuorin's waist, shielding his eyes from the sun with his hand.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Hmm, it's Satiel. Familiar vibes. Yes, I consumed them.
Through memory and essence, Havitz had absorbed every facet of Satan's past.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Well, isn't that terrifying.
Hearing the angels' murderous intent through divine frequency, Havitz nodded in mock appreciation.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Such pure resentment. That's the problem with angels—they need everything to be so absolute.
Satiel glared fiercely.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: Y-you…!
Havitz extended his tongue mockingly, moving it up and down as if licking the distant Satiel.
Satiel's divine aura trembled violently.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: You worm!
Havitz chuckled.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Heh heh heh. Ahh, you're just too fun!
Even as he chuckled, Havitz exaggerated his tongue's grotesque movements.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: I'll disintegrate you!
Satiel's divine aura expanded into a radiant halo, and the angels in the sky assumed an attack formation.

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: Please, restrain yourself.
Satiel's divine aura retracted into a sphere.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: Silence. A mere human dares give me orders? Should I kill you first?
You're welcome to, but causing a commotion here would only please Ikael, wouldn't it?

blocks.speaker: King Gith
blocks.content: Yes. The glowing halo retracted into a sphere.
Satan is also part of the plan. Trust me—Ikael will suffer far more than you could imagine.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: Ikael…
Grinding her teeth audibly, Satiel turned her back and headed toward the main hall.

blocks.speaker: Satiel
blocks.content: I won't wait long. Bring me Ikael, and make her shed tears of blood.
Understood.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: One mountain after another…
As the lesser angels gradually disappeared, the foreign dignitaries exchanged grim looks.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Satiel has gathered over 300 lesser angels. With Mara added to the equation, this might be the most powerful single nation's force we've ever faced.
These weren't the humans of old.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The global population has dropped by a third. Thirty nations have vanished from the map. With core personnel lacking, there are hardly any superpowers left.
Lupist turned to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Lupist
blocks.content: Other nations have strong cards to play. Tormia has its strengths, but your convictions may not hold up against this.
Shirone's cold, calculating eyes sharpened.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A total war.
In the distance, the Truth Pyramid shimmered faintly on the horizon.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Argan. Frigi.
As Shirone's companions exchanged puzzled glances, two apostles immediately knelt before him.

blocks.speaker: Apostle
blocks.content: Yes, Messiah.
Head to Jive. Immediately.

blocks.speaker: Apostle
blocks.content: What?
It was unclear why he had changed the destination so abruptly, especially with the Truth Pyramid so close.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why…?
I've located the angels. All twelve apostles will be summoned there. Follow my instructions when you arrive.

blocks.speaker: Apostle
blocks.content: At the mention of angels, the apostles' expressions hardened.
Understood.
With those words, the apostles leaped into the air, transforming into a dragon and an ice wyrm.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Right, they're dragons
Ares and his party were reminded of their true nature.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Will we be okay without them? The Truth Pyramid is an unknown, mysterious space.
With Lucia's Emotion Sickness temporarily alleviated, the next priority was safety.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: If you're scared, go back. We've done just fine without dragons.
Glen didn't back down.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: I'm saying we need to be prepared. Losing key forces can be a critical issue in any situation. I thought magicians were supposed to be smart, but apparently not all of them.
Their tension from the earlier Nag-Nak Oasis incident threatened to reignite.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What did you say?
And you? When your lover was—

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Tch, forget it.
He, too, had someone precious—Arin.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: Recognizing this, Glen softened but still felt the need to clarify.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: It's true that Yahweh helped Lucia. I'd even risk my life to repay that. But I won't forsake my faith. I still deny him.
Begrudgingly, Glen acknowledged Canis.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Who cares? Got it.
While expecting compensation might seem petty, it wasn't something anyone could do.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: ...Still, he's a magician.
As Canis muttered and returned to his seat, Arin smiled and took his hand.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: You did well. Please, control your temper.
Their mental channel resonated with soft words.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What? I wasn't even that bad. Sure, I get it, though.
If Arin had been in Lucia's place, the thought would've been unbearable.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Huhuhu.
As Arin gazed at Canis with a soft laugh, she leaned in and kissed him lightly on the cheek in front of everyone.
Canis's face turned as red as a tomato, prompting Shirone to chuckle and turn his gaze elsewhere.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Still as inseparable as ever.
Though Shirone hadn't expected Canis to get so flustered over just a kiss on the cheek, he mused:

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They're even worse than me.
Glen, who had been watching enviously, quickly averted his gaze, only to be caught by Lucia, who raised an eyebrow at him.

blocks.speaker: Glen
blocks.content: What are you looking at?
Huh? Oh, nothing, really!

blocks.speaker: Lucia
blocks.content: Liar.
Though there was no doubt about their affection for each other, as priests, they were clumsy in expressing it.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: There's this kind of love, and that kind of love.
Ah, yes. I suppose so.
A brief silence followed, growing more awkward by the second, until Ares leaned closer and asked in a hushed tone:

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Our little sister is doing 'just fine,' isn't she?
Shirone stammered, unsure of how to define 'just fine', prompting Ares to burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Relax, it's a joke! I can tell just by looking. But as her brother, let me warn you: once Karmis sets her sights on something, she won't stop until she's the best.
As Ares walked toward the Truth Pyramid, Shirone stared after him with an exasperated expression.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What kind of brother is he?
Meanwhile, the couples—Canis and Arin, Glen and Lucia—held hands and followed after Ares.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There were all kinds of love.
I just hope everyone can be happy.
With that thought, Shirone trailed behind them, while the stoic Euphra and Zulu brought up the rear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Whoa…
The massive structure loomed overhead, its sheer size overwhelming the human spirit.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis, this… This is…
The massive structure loomed overhead, its sheer size overwhelming the human spirit.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: What does it look like to you?
Though Arin perceived every object as if seeing it for the first time, her reaction now was extreme.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: I don't know. What is this? A massive light, but scattered with black specks like noise…
The light and darkness seemed mixed, but not in harmony.

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: The Truth Pyramid has existed since before human civilization. The ancient Kingdom of Paras modeled countless pyramids after it, meant to serve as tombs for their kings.
Shirone added

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ancient civilizations often erased their own records, so even Omega contained no data about them.
We don't know how much they understood about the outside world,

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: but considering Paras was the birthplace of astrology, it's hard to dismiss the possibility entirely.
A brief silence followed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Astrology…
The power to transcend time.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Done.
After finishing his ritual, Ares picked up his backpack.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Let's head in. Whatever it is, we'll figure it out by exploring. Maybe we'll even meet God.
Oh, merciful God,

blocks.speaker: Glen and Lucia
blocks.content: murmured as Shirone approached the pyramid.
Though small compared to the overall structure, there was a gap large enough for them to enter.
Surprisingly, the interior wasn't dark.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Strange…
When Ares turned off his lantern, it became dimmer but still visible.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: There's no light source, so how can we see?
Electrons, probably,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Electrons in the air are faintly emitting light. It's unsettling.
His skin prickled with a strange sensation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But electrons can't emit light without energy. Could it be…?
Yes. The pyramid itself might be generating energy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is that possible? This is an artifact from before recorded history.
It's likely similar to OOPArts. Everything from ancient civilizations seems to generate electricity.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then it's essentially a perpetual energy source?
By this world's standards, yes. More importantly, OOPArts often emit electromagnetic fields that disrupt brainwaves. They might even erase data from the Akashic Records. Be cautious.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Understood. Everyone, stay alert.
Despite Ares's attempt at humor, the group's mood remained tense.
Navigating the maze-like passages, Canis surveyed their surroundings with a grim expression.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It's massive.
The overwhelming scale they had felt outside was nothing compared to the experience of being inside.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: If we tried to explore it all, we'd never finish. But the problem is, there's no sign of civilization in any of these rooms.
This was typical of ancient civilizations.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Perhaps it's designed to control some sort of flow?
A complex path. Rooms of varying sizes. Irregular patterns. If the entire structure is meant to create an invisible flow…

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Then that's how it generates electricity. Hmm…
As Ares mused, Arin, glancing back, suddenly widened her eyes.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis, look over there.
Blue glowing symbols on the walls of the corridor began to shine and move toward them.
